_description: Published baseline numbers from V2X-ViTv2 paper (TPAMI 2025, Xu et al.), Table II. These are taken directly from the paper and used as comparison rows in our Section 7 tables. Source: arXiv:2403.07729v2, Table II. Numbers may vary by ±0.005 depending on the paper version.
_source: V2X-ViTv2: Improved Vision Transformers for Vehicle-to-Everything Cooperative Perception. IEEE TPAMI, 47(1):650-663, Jan 2025. Table II.
_citation: @article{xu2024v2x, title={V2X-ViTv2: Improved Vision Transformers for Vehicle-to-Everything Cooperative Perception}, author={Xu, Runsheng and Chen, Chia-Ju and Tu, Zhengzhong and Yang, Ming-Hsuan}, journal={IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence}, volume={47}, number={1}, pages={650--663}, year={2025}, publisher={IEEE}}

V2XSet_Perfect._note: Perfect setting (no noise, no time delay). From V2X-ViTv2 Table II left half.

V2XSet_Noisy._note: Noisy setting (0.2m pos noise, 0.2 deg heading, 100ms time delay). From V2X-ViTv2 Table II right half.

DAIR-V2X._note: Real-world DAIR-V2X dataset. From V2X-ViTv2 Table III. Note: DiscoNet, Where2Comm, How2Comm, V2X-ViT v1, V2X-ViT v2 all evaluate on the same 80m range. Our experiments on DAIR-V2X will use the same setting.

Robustness_V2XSet_Noisy._note: Noise robustness comparison at P_A=0.2 (most aggressive compression). Numbers from V2X-ViTv2 Table IV (synthesized from paper text; some may need updating).

_what_we_must_beat._comment: To claim our method works, our SemRD-V2X at P_A=0.5 (50% bandwidth) should be COMPARABLE to (slightly worse than, but not far from) the methods below. Better to compare against strong baselines that emphasize communication efficiency.
